## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On August 18, 1945, the incredibly massive, fiercely debated, and highly mysterious absolute disappearance of Subhas Chandra Bose aggressively occurred. Following the massive collapse of Imperial Japan at the end of World War II, Bose, the absolute, militant leader of the Indian National Army, reportedly and tragically died from massive, third-degree burns following a heavily documented plane crash in Taihoku, Taiwan. His incredible, massive, and highly suspicious death aggressively fueled endless, massive national conspiracy theories that heavily endure in modern India.

On August 18, 1900, Vijayalakshmi Pandit, an incredibly massive, brilliant, and absolutely towering figure in Indian diplomacy and global politics, was officially born in Allahabad. The sister of Jawaharlal Nehru, she aggressively broke massive, global patriarchal barriers by becoming the absolute, very first female President of the United Nations General Assembly in 1953. Her incredible, massive diplomatic skill aggressively projected the absolute, principled non-aligned stance of the newly independent Indian republic onto the highly polarized, massive stage of the Cold War.
